summary: Stuttered into the tournament via the play-offs under Graham Potter (appointed October, with Sweden at a low ebb after finishing bottom of their qualifying group on one point). Potter prefers possession but, needing results, opted for a pragmatic, defensively solid system — fronted by two of the world's most expensive strikers.
style: Conservative 3-4-2-1 with a five-man defence — shift across compactly to close space and double up on wingers (beat Ukraine in the play-off on 32% possession). Straightforward attack: play long to the muscular Gyökeres with the pacy Elanga flitting around him to pounce on knock-downs, and break into the big spaces their forwards attack so well.

counterReliance.note: Defensive block, then direct and counter into space

strengths: Compact, organised low block that's hard to open up
Elite striker pair attacking big spaces on the counter
Reacted well to simpler instructions — solid and clear
Added Spurs set-piece coach Andreas Georgson
weaknesses: Cedes possession by design; limited build-up sophistication for now
Confidence and form were fragile heading into qualification's play-off route

keyPlayers.name: Alexander Isak
keyPlayers.note: British-record £125m signing — fluid movement, quick feet, mastery of tight spaces; injury-hit first season but nearing fitness (scored vs Norway)

keyPlayers.name: Viktor Gyökeres
keyPlayers.note: £55m battering-ram — powerful, direct target for the long ball and a counter finisher

keyPlayers.name: Anthony Elanga
keyPlayers.note: Pace to flit around Gyökeres and pounce on knock-downs; clinical on the break

tactics: Defend deep and compact, double up wide, then go long to Gyökeres or break into space for Isak and Elanga. Potter will add complexity later, but expect the simple, solid structure that rescued the campaign to stay.
notes: Snuck into the play-offs on Nations League form despite a one-point qualifying group.
sources: The Athletic — 2026 World Cup tactical guide, Group F (5 Jun 2026)
John Walker (@johnwalker_1986) — pre-tournament tactical scouting, May–Jun 2026
